word_list.word: beware of a silent dog and still water
word_list.pronunciation: bɪwˈɛɹ əvə sˈaɪlənt dˈɑːɡ ænd stˈɪl wˈɔːɾɚ

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is important to be wary of situations that appear calm and quiet on the surface, as they may conceal hidden dangers or threats

examples.x: "In a relationship, beware of a silent dog and still water, as they may indicate underlying issues or unexpressed emotions that can lead to conflict," said the couples therapist during the counseling session.

senses.definition: used to imply that being informed or aware of a potential danger or problem in advance allows one to be better prepared to deal with it

examples.x: It’s good that you told me about the storm—forewarned is forearmed.
I’m glad you mentioned the traffic; forewarned is forearmed when planning the trip.
She felt better prepared for the exam after receiving some tips—forewarned is forearmed.
Forewarned is forearmed, so I made sure to bring an umbrella after hearing about the rain.
It’s always helpful to know what to expect—forewarned is forearmed in every situation.

word_list.word: {not} near the water until you learn how to swim
word_list.pronunciation: nˌɑːt ɡˌoʊ nˌɪɹ ðə wˈɔːɾɚɹ ʌntˈɪl juː lˈɜːn hˌaʊ tə swˈɪm

senses.definition: used to emphasize that it is important to be prepared and have the necessary skills before engaging in a potentially dangerous or risky activity

word_list.word: beware of the young doctor and the old barber
word_list.pronunciation: bɪwˈɛɹ ʌvðə jˈʌŋ dˈɑːktɚ ænd ðɪ ˈoʊld bˈɑːɹbɚ

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is important to be aware of potential risks or shortcomings when dealing with individuals who may lack experience or who may be past their prime

examples.x: The job seeker's friend warned her to remember the saying 'beware of the young doctor and the old barber' when she considered accepting a job offer from a start-up with little track record or an established company with outdated practices.

word_list.word: (one shall|) drive gently over the stones
word_list.pronunciation: wˈʌn ʃˌæl dɹˈaɪv dʒˈɛntli ˌoʊvɚ ðə stˈoʊnz

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is important to be cautious and gentle in one's approach, particularly in situations where there may be potential obstacles or challenges

word_list.word: it is all fun and games until someone loses an eye
word_list.pronunciation: ɪt ɪz ˈɔːl fˈʌn ænd ɡˈeɪmz ʌntˈɪl sˈʌmwʌn lˈuːzᵻz ɐn ˈaɪ

senses.definition: used to suggest that even if a situation may seem harmless or amusing, it is important to take proper precautions and be mindful of potential risks or dangers to prevent serious harm or injury

word_list.pronunciation: bɪwˈɛɹ ʌv ðə ɡɹˈiːks bˈɛɹɪŋ ɡˈɪfts

senses.definition: used to advise people to be cautious of accepting gifts from those who may have ulterior motives or hidden intentions, especially when the gift giver may be an adversary or enemy

examples.x: When the rival company sent a gift to the CEO, he remembered the saying beware of Greeks bearing gifts and declined the offer, suspecting that they might have had an ulterior motive.

word_list.pronunciation: biː kˈɛɹfəl wˌʌt juː wˈɪʃ fɔːɹ

senses.definition: used to warn people to think carefully about their desires, as the things they wish for may have unforeseen and unintended consequences

word_list.word: he who sups with the devil should have a long spoon
word_list.pronunciation: hiː hˌuː sˈʌps wɪððə dˈɛvəl ʃˌʊdəv ɐ lˈɑːŋ spˈuːn

senses.definition: used to imply that when dealing with morally corrupt or untrustworthy individuals, one should be cautious and take measures to protect oneself from potential harm or negative consequences

word_list.word: kindle not a fire you cannot put out
word_list.pronunciation: kˈɪndəl nˌɑːɾə fˈaɪɚ juː kænˈɑːt pˌʊt ˈaʊt

senses.definition: used to suggest that it is important to take precautions and avoid situations that may quickly become out of control or difficult to manage

word_list.word: {not} (test|measure) the depth of (water|river) with both feet
word_list.pronunciation: nˌɑːt tˈɛst mˈɛʒɚ ðə dˈɛpθ ʌv wˈɔːɾɚ ɹˈɪvɚ wɪð bˈoʊθ fˈiːt

senses.definition: used to suggest that one should be cautious and take measured steps when approaching a new or unfamiliar situation
